chapter: Chapter: Parliament and the Making of Laws

questions.question: What is Parliament?
questions.explanation: Parliament is the institution that makes laws for the country.

questions.question: Which body makes laws in India?
questions.explanation: Parliament is responsible for making laws in India.

questions.question: Which house of Parliament represents the people of India?
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha members are directly elected by the people.

questions.question: Which house of Parliament represents the states?
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha represents the states of India.

questions.question: Who elects members of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Citizens elect their representatives in the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Who is the head of the Indian government?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister leads the government and the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: Which institution checks the work of the government?
questions.explanation: Parliament discusses and checks government actions.

questions.question: What do Members of Parliament (MPs) represent?
questions.explanation: MPs represent the people who elect them.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of Parliament?
questions.explanation: One of the main functions of Parliament is making laws.

questions.question: What is a law?

options.A: A rule made by the government

questions.explanation: Laws are rules created to maintain order in society.

questions.question: Which document is discussed before becoming a law?
questions.explanation: A bill is proposed in Parliament before it becomes a law.

questions.question: Who debates laws in Parliament?
questions.explanation: MPs discuss and debate proposed laws.

questions.question: Which election chooses representatives to Parliament?
questions.explanation: General elections are held to elect MPs.

questions.question: Which house of Parliament has more power in financial matters?
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha has greater control over money matters.

questions.question: Who is the constitutional head of India?
questions.explanation: The President is the constitutional head of the country.

questions.question: What is the term of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Members of Lok Sabha are elected for five years.

questions.question: What is the role of opposition in Parliament?
questions.explanation: Opposition parties question and check government actions.

questions.question: Who proposes new laws in Parliament?
questions.explanation: Ministers introduce bills in Parliament.

questions.question: Which activity takes place in Parliament?
questions.explanation: Parliament holds debates and discussions on national issues.

questions.question: Why is Parliament important in a democracy?
questions.explanation: Parliament represents citizens and makes laws for them.

questions.question: Which group forms the government after elections?
questions.explanation: The party with majority seats forms the government.

questions.question: What does MP stand for?
questions.explanation: MP means Member of Parliament.

questions.question: Which process allows citizens to choose representatives?
questions.explanation: Elections allow citizens to choose their representatives.

questions.question: What ensures accountability of government in Parliament?
questions.explanation: Debates and questioning help hold the government accountable.

questions.question: Which system of government does India follow?
questions.explanation: India follows a parliamentary democratic system.
